axis_a.name: dispatch surface (main() invocation coverage of the 9-check matrix)

data.extra_check_beyond_topic: C10-raw98-taxonomy (post-topic addition; does not break F1 since the 9 topic checks are all present)

axis_b.name: per-rule scope semantics (registration-moment vs full-corpus audit)

data.scope_effectiveness: scoped fail_count(0) < full_repo fail_count(8) — --target-rule narrows correctly
data.interpretation: post-strengthening commit 773c12d98 closed C4 (raw 71 falsifier ≥3) — the SPECIFIC chronology gap the kick topic targets — but raw 92 still has compliance debt across 5 other checks. The chronology gap is closed on the falsifier-axis; broader rule-completeness backfill belongs to raw 87 sync + raw 95 triad-mandate ramps.

axis_c.name: selftest coverage (instability surface — shape contract gap)

data.instability_implication: selftest is a shape contract on the 3-element [check-id, exit-code, verdict] return tuple. 7 of the 10 sub-checks have NO shape contract. A regression that returns a 2-element array from any inline scanner would not be caught by selftest — it would crash at _emit_check_row instead.

data.missing_invocation_mode: harness-level PreToolUse hook on Write/Edit of .raw / .own / .roadmap
data.implication: Even though the binary is stable and the 9-check matrix is dispatched correctly, an author writing directly to .raw via the harness Write tool can register a new rule without invoking the lint. raw 92's chronology gap is structurally analogous: the gate exists in the corpus but no runtime enforcement blocks the write. Closing the chronology gap fully requires the hook wiring follow-up (raw 97 follow-up #2).

fan_out.correlation_method: axis_a (dispatch source code) vs axis_b (runtime scope semantics) ~0.45 (both anchored to source but axis_b adds runtime exec); axis_a vs axis_c (selftest body) ~0.30 (same file but disjoint function bodies — selftest is independent shape contract); axis_a vs axis_d (settings.json) ~0.05 (entirely different file/system); axis_b vs axis_c ~0.20; axis_b vs axis_d ~0.15; axis_c vs axis_d ~0.10. Pairwise mean ≈ 0.21 — well below 0.7 threshold.

tier_1_gate_via_falsifier_pass_pattern.promotion_rule: ≥5 PASS out of 7 with no PASS fabricated (raw 91 C3) AND each FAIL maps to a tracked obstruction (O1, O2)

options_considered.label: implement dispatch + scope + chronology closure + selftest 10/10 coverage + PreToolUse hook wiring

axes_surfaced.decision: C primary; D residual delegated to raw 97 follow-ups
axes_surfaced.rationale: The kick topic names raw 92 type chronology gap as the closure target. F6 PASS (C4-raw71-falsifier ok on raw:92 scope) confirms the precise axis is closed. The 5-check residual on raw 92 (axis_b data) belongs to compliance ramps under raw 87 + raw 95, not to this stabilization cycle. Selftest gap (axis_c) and hook gap (axis_d) are documented as obstructions O1, O2 — they do not block this cycle's PASS verdict because the gate spec is registration-moment-only and the dispatch surface is complete + production-verified by F1+F2+F5.

axes_surfaced.name: raw 92 chronology gap — what specifically is being closed

options_considered.label: retroactively rewrite raw 92 first-registration commit to inline 3 falsifiers
options_considered.rejected_rationale: git history rewrite is destructive; raw 49 additive-before-destructive forbids; chronology gap is an immutable historical artifact

options_considered.label: extend raw 97 retroactive scope to pre-existing entries
options_considered.rejected_rationale: violates raw 97's own scope clause ('REGISTRATION MOMENT only', .raw 2196); diagnose-cycle 82a59c5f F4 PASS confirmed grandfather pattern

options_considered.label: ship the gate binary + per-rule scope so any FUTURE post-raw-71 raw cannot register without falsifiers AND the lint can attest raw 92 itself now satisfies C4 post-strengthening
options_considered.rejected_rationale: selected — this is what F6 verifies; the chronology gap is closed by binary existence + production verification on the raw 92 scope, not by retroactive history rewrite

options_considered.label: add raw 92-specific exemption clause to the lint (whitelist legacy)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: introduces special-casing; dilutes the gate; raw 71's grandfather pattern at status-class level is the canonical path

axes_surfaced.rationale: The gap is closed in the forward direction (the lint now exists and would block a re-occurrence) and in the spot-verification direction (F6 PASS confirms raw 92's C4 verdict is ok against the live lint). The kick topic deliberately scopes 'raw 92 type chronology gap' — the type, not raw 92 specifically — meaning future analogues are now caught at registration moment by the running gate binary.

options_considered.rejected_rationale: selected as residual — promoted to obstruction O1; not blocking because shape regressions surface at runtime via _emit_check_row crash, not silently

options_considered.label: audit ledger uncapped — state/raw_registration_audit/audit.jsonl will grow unbounded

options_considered.label: delegated tools (raw_english_only_lint, triad_lint, raw_taxonomy_lint, honesty_triad_lint) skip-on-absence — if any deletes, gate silently passes that check

axes_surfaced.decision: A+B+C+D all logged as obstructions; none individually blocks this cycle PASS
axes_surfaced.rationale: The kick topic is stabilization of the 9-check dispatch — that surface is verified. Each residual risk has a clearly-scoped follow-up cycle (raw 97 follow-ups #2-#4 + selftest extension as a new follow-up). The honest accounting is: gate works for the named purpose (raw 92 chronology axis); gate has known runtime-coverage gaps that are not in this kick's scope.

obstructions.title: selftest coverage gap — 3/10 sub-checks have shape contracts; 7 inline scanners are not selftest-validated
obstructions.evidence: /Users/ghost/core/hive/tool/raw_registration_gate_lint.hexa:467-491 (_cmd_selftest invokes _check_raw2 + _check_raw33 + _check_raw98 only); println at line 489 explicitly self-labels 'skeleton — 3 shape checks PASS'
obstructions.impact: A regression that returns a 2-element array from any of {_check_raw43, _check_raw71, _check_raw75, _check_raw87, _check_raw91, _check_raw93, _check_raw95} would not be caught by selftest — it would crash at _emit_check_row[1] / [2] index-out-of-range during a real run. F3 FAIL.
obstructions.suggested_close: extend _cmd_selftest with shape assertions for the 7 missing inline scanners; run against a synthetic in-memory rule fixture (no need for real .raw access). New cycle: 'tool/raw_registration_gate_lint.hexa selftest 10/10 coverage extension'.

obstructions.title: PreToolUse hook NOT YET wired in ~/.claude/settings.json — runtime gate inactive at harness layer
obstructions.impact: Author writing directly to .raw / .own / .roadmap via Write/Edit tools bypasses the gate. Binary stability + dispatch correctness are necessary but not sufficient for runtime enforcement. F4 FAIL.
obstructions.suggested_close: raw 97 follow-up #2 (.raw 2233): wire ~/.claude/settings.json#PreToolUse on Write|Edit of .raw|.own|.roadmap to invoke hexa tool/raw_registration_gate_lint.hexa --strict --target-rule from-diff. Separate cycle scope: harness-wiring kick.

obstructions.title: audit ledger uncapped — state/raw_registration_audit/audit.jsonl grows unbounded
obstructions.evidence: _audit_append (line 544-560) appends without rotation; no truncate/cron/lifecycle policy in tool
obstructions.impact: long-running adoption will accumulate unbounded JSONL rows. Not a correctness blocker but a long-term ops debt.
obstructions.suggested_close: raw 97 follow-up #4 (audit-ledger cron rotation): nightly logrotate with 90-day retention; emit aggregate metrics to state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl.

obstructions.title: delegated-tool absence-skip — if any of {raw_english_only_lint, triad_lint, raw_taxonomy_lint, honesty_triad_lint} is deleted, the gate SILENTLY passes that check
obstructions.evidence: _check_raw33 line 94-96 + _check_raw91 (full-repo branch) line 117-119 + _check_raw95 line 153-155 + _check_raw98 line 169-171 all `return [..., '0', 'skip']` when _file_exists returns false
obstructions.impact: If a refactor accidentally renames or moves any delegated tool, the gate's verdict would shift from fail to skip without alerting — silent coverage regression.
obstructions.suggested_close: promote skip → fail when classifier-version >= v2 AND specific delegated tool is named in the rule body's enforce-layer (i.e., the rule's own declaration says 'requires triad_lint'). Add CI assert on tool-presence list. Track as separate hardening cycle.

fixpoint_marker: stabilization-converged for the kick topic's named axis: 9-check matrix dispatch IS implemented and verified; raw 92 chronology gap IS closed on the C4 raw 71 falsifier axis. Residual gaps (selftest 7-check coverage, PreToolUse hook, audit ledger cron, delegated-tool absence-skip semantic) are documented as obstructions O1-O4 with named successor cycles. NO new raw rule needed; NO source code change to the lint needed in this cycle; the gate spec + binary + per-rule scope are stable.
